rulings.Case Title: MALCOLM DRILLING COMPANY, INC. VS. SWINERTON WEBCOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT HAITONG INTERNATIONAL EQUITY INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT LIMITED [FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE CGC-21-591319; CGC-21-594098 AND CGC-22-598031) MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is GRANTED. The purported October 3, 2024 service is quashed. Herrick Corporation's Cross-Complaint as to the moving cross-defendant is dismissed due to the failure to timely serve Summons and Cross-Complaint. Cross-Complainant failed to show that the stay of litigation pending arbitration with other parties' affected or prevented service of the parties that have not yet been served as of the date of the order. Moving party did not generally appear on the cross-complaint and did not waive any requirements regarding proper service.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MURKELLEY, LLC, VS. MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF MURKELLEY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is continued to January 17, 2025. No later than January 2, 2025 moving party to file supplemental evidence showing moving party's efforts to mitigate its losses as is discussed in the Complaint filed on February 28, 2023 (e.g. paragraph 16, 37, 38) and in the complaint filed on August 1, 2023 (e.g. paragraphs 16, 37, 38) and the results of such efforts. No later than January 10, 2025 Defendant may file a response to the evidence that should be limited to Defendant's evidence and Defendant's objections to moving party's evidence. There shall be no further argument from either side. Opposing party shall provide properly bound (stapled or fastened) and tabbed courtesy copies no later than December 31, 2024 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The Court received unbound stacks of paper that are not usable as courtesy copies for reading. Opposing party shall pick up from the outbox its unbound courtesy copies to be bound/tabbed and re-submitted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE BARRETT WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Plaintiffs' Request For An Order To Show Cause And For Issuance Of Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBIN Notice Of Hearing For Plaintiffs' Request For An Order To Show Cause And For Issuance Of Injunction is GRANTED. The Defendants are enjoined from selling, transferring (including to each other), or otherwise disposing of the parcels or real property identified in this motion. Defendants are enjoined from placing any additional voluntary encumbrances (e.g. mortgage secured by a Deed of Trust; failure to pay contractors performing work after the date of this order resulting in a mechanic's lien, etc.) on the property. Defendants shall not be in violation of this order should the property become subject to encumbrances, such as a tax lien. Defendants may seek a partial dissolution of the injunction for the purposes of refinancing the property if the new amount does not exceed the current encumbrances on the property. (e.g. the current mortgage of "$247,747.96" to be refinanced). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
